mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after 2018.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during late-cycle expansions signal capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that erode profit margins, forcing negative S&P 500 adjustments over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the regime-conditional effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 2.0 percent trigger aggressive monetary tightening expectations that increase discount rates and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent, or the threshold effect shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or null correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts combined with rising consumer prices signal overheating demand that forces central banks to tighten policy, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and CPIAUCSL signal yields positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 index baseline indicates severe output contraction that triggers recession pricing and forces immediate negative S&P 500 adjustments within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of cases where INDPRO drops below 100, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.
